# Extração: 01-Nooroo In Feed Medium Length VSL 1

- **Fonte:** 01-Nooroo In Feed Medium Length VSL 1.docx

Say goodbye to edema in your legs.



Try this trick tonight and see what happens.



Here are three early signs of edema.



One is random swelling that comes and goes, especially at the end of the day or after physical activity.



Two, feeling of heaviness or tightness.



Your legs suddenly feel like they're stuck in the mud.



This sensation can occur even without any visible changes.



And three, restricted range of motion.



As fluid starts to accumulate, there may be a slight decrease in flexibility or range of motion.



If that sounds like you, you need to hear this.



This is the best way to relieve edema and swelling fast.



It doesn't matter whether the swelling is in your thighs, calves, ankles, or feet.



It doesn't matter whether you've been struggling for three months or three years.



Nor does it matter the size of your legs.



Over 100,000 Americans use it to relieve edema, swelling, and stiffness in their legs quickly and naturally.



This breakthrough solution has already become a total social media sensation.



Hi, my name is Dr. Jeremy Campbell and I'm a doctor of physical therapy from Chicago.



I have over 10 years of clinical experience and clocked in well over 10,000 hours.



Throughout my career, I've helped over 1,100 patients overcome all kinds of foot problems.



Lymphedema, edema, neuropathy, plantar fasciitis.



You name it, I've seen it all.



Anything from mild stiffness and swelling to people struggling with varicose spider veins to edema so bad that you can barely recognize what body part you're looking at.



But it wasn't until earlier this year that I truly cracked the code to provide relief from edema.



It all started with one breakthrough discovery I had.



If you're struggling with swollen legs, feet, or ankles, you may have something called venous insufficiency.



It's a condition where the veins in your calves fail to send the oxygen-starved blood back up to your heart.



This results in blood pooling around the area of your calves, feet, and ankles, which ultimately causes swelling, stiffness, and soreness.



It can happen when you sit at your desk for too many hours every day, but it can also happen when you stand for too long as well.



Your skin may feel like it's overstretched and about to burst any second, making every step heavier than the last, like your legs are stuck in the mud.



But you're not alone.



Recent research shows that over 25 million individuals in the U.S. alone struggle with venous insufficiency, and this number increases every year.



But venous insufficiency is only half the answer.



Here's a little-known fact that very few people know about, but everyone should know about their edema.



What if I told you that the edema and swelling in your feet and legs are actually caused by your calves?



That's right.



Your feet might be perfectly fine.



Your calves are the problem.



Let me explain this further.



Did you know that we doctors refer to calves as the second heart?



That's because they play a crucial role in your body's blood circulation.



You see, your real heart sends blood all over your body, including your feet.



Getting the blood to go down to our feet is easy.



It's like sliding down a slide.



But getting it back up?



It's like climbing back up the slide.



Your heart can't pull all the blood back up by itself.



That's where the calves help with this big job.



When your calf muscles contract, they compress the vein valves in your legs and push the blood from your feet back up towards your heart.



That's why we call the calves the second heart.



They single-handedly maintain normal blood circulation in your lower body.



Now here's the problem that I don't see anyone talking about.



As you sit for long hours every day, continue to age, and become less physically active, your calves get weaker.



As they get weaker, they don't contract the way they used to.



So the vein valves in your calves that are responsible for pushing the blood back up to your heart tend to weaken as well.



This causes the venous insufficiency I mentioned earlier.



When that happens, your calves can no longer send all the old stale blood from your lower body back to your heart.



So some of it stays stuck in your feet and legs.



Now this buildup of old stale blood can cause swelling in your feet and ankles.



But it can also lead to more serious foot conditions if left untreated.



Starting to see how this all fits together?



Weak calves cause your vein valves to weaken.



Those weakened vein valves fail to send the old deoxygenated blood back up to your heart.



Which ultimately causes the blood to pool around your calves, feet, and ankles.



Making your legs swollen and puffy.



And feeling like they're stuck in the mud.



Most people don't even notice that they have early symptoms.



But it's actually the leading cause of why people over 35 struggle with stiff and swollen legs.



And heaviness, described as having legs stuck in concrete.



And while most of the wannabe solutions are trying to relieve swelling by focusing on the feet, they totally miss the fact that it's the weak calves that are the hidden culprit.



And that's also why popular remedies like compression socks, salt baths, and different pills can't solve the problem.



They don't focus on stimulating your calves at all and the vein valves inside of them.



So if you want to get relief from edema in your feet and legs, and if you want to reduce the swelling, stiffness, and aches, you need to stimulate your calves and the vein valves in your calves to reduce the buildup of the pooled blood in your calves, ankles, and feet.



But how do you do that?



Most experts will tell you that you need to move or even start exercising.



That's great advice.



Don't get me wrong.



Physical activity does wonders for stimulating the vein valves in your calves.



But let's be honest.



When you struggle with edema and your feet are all swollen and painful, moving around, let alone running or exercising, is just not possible.



So you end up in this vicious cycle of agony.



Your legs are swollen and painful.



And the best way to relieve it is to activate your muscles, which can only be done by moving around.



But you can't move because your legs are painful and swollen.



So your feet and legs continue to expand like balloons ready to pop.



See how it works?



That's why most people never really solve this problem and are stuck bedridden with painful oversized clown feet and legs their entire lives.



Luckily, there's now a better way.



This is the quickest way to relieve edema and swelling in your legs.



It doesn't require exercising and it doesn't matter how old you are or how bad your current condition is.



And it doesn't matter if you've already tried everything else with no results.



This simple, affordable, doctor-approved bedtime ritual that you can do at home has the power to stimulate the vein valves in your calves and relieve the stubborn swelling as if you let the air out of a balloon.



It has nothing to do with existing solutions like compression socks, elevating your feet, using salt baths, or taking water pills.



This is completely new, completely different, and completely unlike anything else out there.



I didn't even know that this solution existed.



But then one night while going through some old research studies, I went down a rabbit hole and stumbled upon a series of studies on the topic of neuromuscular electrical stimulation, or NMES for short.



A review published in the Journal of Rehabilitation Medicine discussed studies that explored the use of NMES for reducing edema in various clinical settings, including chronic venous and lymphatic conditions.



The results across these studies were positive, demonstrating that NMES could reduce limb swelling and improve quality of life.



So what is NMES?



NMES is a technology that uses gentle electrical stimulation to contract and defibrillate your weak calf muscles.



This forces the vein valves to open and push the old blood to flow away from the extremities and back towards the heart.



It works by emitting targeted low-level electrical impulses at a proven frequency that penetrate deep into your calves.



These impulses cause your calf muscles to alternate between contraction and relaxation, creating a pumping effect that can enhance blood circulation, which ultimately relieves swelling and stiffness in your legs, calves, ankles, and feet.



It works by placing your feet on a pad made from a special conductive material.



This pad is connected to a controller that sends electrical impulses stimulating the muscles in your feet and calves.



Once your feet are properly positioned on the electric foot pad, the controller immediately starts emitting electrical impulses at a specific frequency, causing the muscle tissue to contract and relax repeatedly.



This is specifically designed to mimic the natural pumping of venous systems, which can simulate the muscle activity you would experience during activities like walking, all without you lifting a finger. 



Now, the problem is that most of these devices out there are not user-friendly. 



They often include numerous wires and lack a user manual, making the setup process a nightmare that immediately discourages you from using it. 



But worst of all, because human skin is actually a poor conductor of electricity, the pad must be made from a material that conducts electricity efficiently enough to compensate for this limitation. 



So if you want to experience the benefits of this technology, you normally would have to go to a rehabilitation center. 



However, that's not always optimal. 



You have to pick one that offers it, then you have to go through the process to book a session, then you have to get yourself there driving there and back. 



So it got me thinking, what if I could take the same groundbreaking technology that top doctors use to temporarily reduce that excessive swelling and put it into a simpler, much more affordable device that can be used by everyday people at home? 



This was a herculean task, and I knew I couldn't do it alone. After all, I'm just a doctor. I needed some engineers to build the device. 



So I partnered up with a U.S. biotech startup called Neuro. 



These guys are known for making some of the best pain relief devices that have helped more than 300,000 men and women across America. 



If my idea to make this technology available to everyone was even remotely possible, I knew that these guys were the ones to do it. 



So I went ahead and I got to work. 



We meticulously analyzed the customer and market data and confirmed there's a wide gap between the needs of the patients with edema and currently available solutions. 



We've taken that specific information and built the first three prototypes. 



We shared the prototypes with small cohorts of Neuro's existing customers to see which prototype works the best, if any. 



They all worked, but not as well as I wanted them to. 



So we went ahead and we created three more. 



Those were much better, but they turned out incredibly expensive. 



We kept repeating the same process. 



Months passed by and we had nothing to show for our work. The process was much more expensive than we initially thought, and it was a manufacturing nightmare. I'm not gonna lie. There were times when I thought maybe the current technology was not yet ready for this type of device. 



But then there was this little voice in my head that just didn't allow me to stop. 



I knew that if we pulled this off, it would help thousands of people. 



So we just kept trying, working around the clock. And after another three months of 12 to 14 hour workdays, we did it. 



We created a compact, easy to use, and most importantly, an affordable device that leverages the same enemy as technology. 



Finally, I felt confident that we had built the best ally for battling edema. An ally that doesn't judge or complain. 



One that's available 24 seven, at home, at work, or when you're out and about. 



Giving you the confidence that next time a sudden flare-up of edema tries to hijack your day, you know it can be shut down within minutes. 



Introducing my brainchild, the Neuro NMES Foot Massager. 



The Neuro NMES Foot Massager fully harnesses the power of neuromuscular electrical stimulation technology which helps relieve swelling, stiffness, and pain in your legs, calves, ankles, and feet. It's made from a special soft rubber designed for optimal electrical conductivity, enhancing its effectiveness and comfort. 



Now, if you look a little closer on the mat, it has these little lines in the shape of your feet. 



These little lines are not there only to show you where to place your feet. 



Although, that's helpful information as well. 



But these lines are made of special silver and carbon threads which are great conductive elements. This allows them to create pathways for electrical currents to penetrate into the muscle tissue of your feet and legs. 



From the moment you put your feet on the Neuro NMES Foot Massager, you'll immediately feel powerful electrical impulses spreading through your feet and legs.



These impulses penetrate deep into your muscle tissue, mimicking muscle contraction.



This rhythmic contraction and relaxation of muscle tissue pushes the pooled blood up from your legs.



There are several clinical studies investigating the efficacy of NMES technology in managing the symptoms associated with EMA, and the interest in this area is increasing rapidly every day.



With consistent use, the Neuro NMES Foot Massager can help stimulate your blood circulation in your entire lower body, which can help reduce swelling and stiffness and allow your legs to function normally without limitations.



No more struggling to find shoes that fit comfortably.



No more shame because your legs and feet look like two balloons.



No more pain and discomfort that keeps you trapped in your own body.



I'm telling you right now, there's not a single aspect of your life that won't improve once you start stimulating the vein valves in your calves.



Your work life.



Your social life.



Heck, even your marriage.



Everything's better when you are not held back by edema, pain, and swelling.



And all it takes is 15 minutes per day to notice a difference.



I would go as far as to say that you'll feel the difference the very first time you try it.



Your legs will feel better than they felt in years.
